name: White Chocolate Brownies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Grease and flour an 8-inch square baking pan.
3. Melt the butter and 4 ounces of white chocolate in the top of a double boiler over hot water. Remove from heat and add the remaining white chocolate. Stir until fully melted and combined. Set aside.
4. In a large bowl, beat the eggs. Add sugar and beat for 2-3 minutes until fluffy. Gradually stir in the white chocolate and butter mixture, vanilla extract, and flour until smooth.
5. Fold in the semi-sweet chocolate chips by hand using a spatula—do not overmix.
6. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake for 35 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Allow to cool on a wire rack before cutting into squares or bars.
